Left Eye of Xygoz  
 

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
WIS: +12 INT: +12 MANA: +80 ENDUR: +80
Effect: Ultravision (Worn, Casting Time: Instant)
WT: 1.5 Size: SMALL
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Left Eye of Xygoz
Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
WIS: +12 INT: +12 MANA: +80 ENDUR: +80
Effect: Ultravision (Worn, Casting Time: Instant)
WT: 1.5 Size: SMALL
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Type:Misc
Stackable:No
Submitted By:Thought Heartsong
Lucy Entry By:Rombus
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-01-12 08:19:22
Page Updated:Thu Dec 26th, 2024

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Rarity: Common
Level to Attain: 55

Good but not great
QuoteReply
# Dec 27 2002 at 7:32 PM Rating: Default

I won this the other day, because no one else wanted it and I'm a human.

It's great for traveling, but for fights I use my Tyro's Manuscript.

RE: Good but not great
QuoteReply
# Apr 12 2003 at 1:42 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592255930943
720 posts
thats not true at all, name another item pre velious that has 12 int 12 wis an 80 mana?? it weighs nothing... it is all /all and it is primary or secondary.. i would replace my book of magistrate with that. that thing rocks..
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 03 2001 at 10:21 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Total human monk item, monks should get!
#REDACTED, Posted: Sep 27 2001 at 8:20 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) i don't know about you but this would come in very handy to me... sure i am only a lvl 8 druid... but i have some good stuff and that is a lot better then my chiten shell shield. i would probably keep my velium etched stone mace in the other hand of coure. but i need a lot of mana. that and some better spells but that will just have to wait i guess. by the way... is there a right eye?
RE: i like it
QuoteReply
# Feb 18 2002 at 1:36 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591771236046
32 posts
It would be handy to any caster, but keep in mind it is no drop and dropped off a 60+ dragon. No way you'd get this til 55+. By the time you get this thing you'll have replaced your velium etched stone mace as well.

Thats a pretty goopd thing. especially for a non DE Caster :P
▲ Page top
Its real
QuoteReply
# Sep 26 2001 at 2:49 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591751557929
1 post
Its real I submitted it.

I will send a screenshot of it in.

It looks like an diamond in inventory and looks like a primatic shield when wielded.

It does how ever show up as the same graphic on the primary hand as secondary so it looks like it is inverted (facing in towards the casters body)

if you like log into Vallon and I will come showe it to you. Sending the pic tonight.

I also agree its a nice item for a healer. The mana pool add can really help.

Thought
Vallon Zek
▲ Page top
Easy to 200
QuoteReply
# Sep 26 2001 at 7:53 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591750302620
32 posts
I agree with you, Anbori. After playing a Paladin up to level 35, I decided to make an erudite wizard. I was able to max out his int at level 20 with only about 1kpp in spending, and help on the Wizard's quests like the staff of the wheel.
Currently, my wizard is level 47, and my official Main. ;-) (Paladin is still 37)
▲ Page top
Not extremely useful...
QuoteReply
# Sep 25 2001 at 11:14 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591756055432
345 posts
I've spoken to many high-level pure casters that rely on intelligence, and they've said that intelligence items are very easy to come by. They all keep their INT at 200, and make the rest of their gear raw mana due to the very diminished value of INT higher than 200 (known as a soft cap).

Considering the difficulty of Veeshan's Peak, this is not likely a real item. There are countless items better than this. There was a GM event a while back where a bunch of GMs and players on Test were superbuffed and took on Veeshan's Peak. The end was reached, but all of the approximately 60 people died excepting 5 who were flagged as invulnerable.

Why would a dungeon that difficult have such a petty reward? These dragons don't go down easily. It just doesn't make sense.

-- Anbori <Obsidian Pact>
Scaled Mystic of Tallon Zek
▲ Page top
RE: Not extremely useful...
QuoteReply
# Sep 25 2001 at 4:52 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591756060085
32 posts
Umm actually it is extremely useful.
the fact that many high level pure casters hit the 200 int/wis softcap doesn't negate the extra 80 mana on this item. The combination of high mana and high int could allow someone to replace another slot item with more diverse stats or resists without losing any mana at all.
The ultravision isn't bad either.
▲ Page top
RE: Not extremely useful...
QuoteReply
# Sep 25 2001 at 11:46 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591750174660
Sage
52 posts
It is a real item. Just took a while to show up here.

http://www.gameznet.com/eq/equipment/ss/lefteyeofxygoz.jpg

▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Sep 25 2001 at 1:08 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) It is a fake. Look closely at the image you have pointed to via that link: around the text, there are subtle dithering errors in the background image. This is a dead giveaway for a fake item, friends; when they cut-and-paste the image to make a new item, they can't possibly line up all pixels in the background image correctly, so they smudge them.
RE: Not extremely useful...
QuoteReply
# Sep 25 2001 at 1:52 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1594570556750
57 posts
Transforming images from game format to another creates slight pixel errors on result product.
▲ Page top
RE: Not extremely useful...
QuoteReply
# Sep 25 2001 at 1:39 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591756121988
2 posts
You sir, are a moron.

"Look closely at the image you have pointed to via that link: around the text, there are subtle dithering errors in the background image."

Its called .jpg compression ... it happens on a lot of images on the net, especially if they don't turn compression off when they save the pic as a .jpg.

"Point 2: the image does not include slot information (handheld, back, etc). Sloppy fake work."

Well, if you look at the zone it comes from, you will realise that VP was in the game LONG before they added stuff like slot information to item text. This item was found a long time ago, and just never made it to Allakhazams for some reason. Was found long before they added the info as to what slot items go in.

Its a real item, I assure you of that.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Sep 25 2001 at 5:26 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Think it might be fake.......
nice
QuoteReply
# Sep 25 2001 at 12:51 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592249921043
157 posts
this is a sweet *** piece, at that lvl you aren't meleeing anyways (reffering to the comment about it having no AC). My best friend plays a blind *** Barbarian shaman, and I know he'll blow his load when he see's this piece. 80 mana is the equivalent of 8 wis at 60 and at anything lower then that it's a godsend. This is a friggen NICE piece.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Sep 24 2001 at 9:53 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) well... i want one =)
?
QuoteReply
# Sep 24 2001 at 6:33 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591756036786
22 posts
Does it really matter what class or race it "should" go to? If some of you havent noticed items are usually given away to those on the raid that either
1) have a need for it.
2) have seniority and want it.

Personally, (go ahead and call it a gripe) but whats the point on argueing who "should" get it when your not even there to see it?

anyone else see the point im sort of getting at?

QuoteReply
# Sep 24 2001 at 5:38 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591757464810
156 posts
Unfortunately, the dang thing probably looks like the stupid little totem item when held. Or maybe the blinking red glow. Oh boy.

Hopefully when Luclin comes out they'll have added new graphics for non-weapon held items.

Otherwise, its a great item for just about any caster who's not too interested in anything but Wis/Int on an item.
▲ Page top
RE: Equipped Look?
QuoteReply
# Sep 25 2001 at 1:43 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591756121988
2 posts
Actually this looks like, if I remember correctly, a shimmoring orb. Since it can be equiped in either hand, in primary it looks like an inverted shimmoring orb, since the graphic only bends one way, and was originally made to go in offhand. (you will understand what I mean by inverted shimmmoring orb if you have seen any of the items that use that)
